member.name: Graham Leadbitter
member.role: MP for Moray West, Nairn and Strathspey
member.synopsis: Graham Leadbitter is the Scottish National Party MP for Moray West, Nairn and Strathspey , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the roles of Shadow SNP Spokesperson (Science, Innovation and Technology), Shadow SNP Spokesperson (Transport), and Shadow SNP Spokesperson (Energy Security and Net Zero).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: HM Coastguard: Conditions of Employment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has formally endorsed the recent change to the terms of the Coastguard Rescue Service.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since the Court of Appeal judgment in January this year, which meant that the current model could not legally continue, careful consideration has been given to the legal and operational implications. After careful review and consideration of the options a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Coalsnaughton
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, with reference to the Prime Minister’s contribution during Prime Minister’s Questions on 3 June 2026, Column 1161, when did he or his Ministers contact Clackmannanshire Council.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I exchanged correspondence with Clackmannanshire Council on 3rd and 4th June and I am meeting with the Council and other partners on 9th June. The Mining Remediation Authority has been on site and working with the Council and other multi-agency partners s...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Heating: Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what financial assistance his Department is providing to households who are unable to have a heat pump or heat battery, to ensure a fair transition to low carbon heating.

writtenQuestions.heading: Heating: Housing

writtenQuestions.answerText: Decarbonising heating in Scotland is largely devolved, allowing the Scottish Government to set its own policies and support schemes. Scottish households can access support through the Home Energy Scotland Grant and Loan scheme, including grants to replace...

writtenQuestions.heading: Heating
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether he has made an assessment of the potential merits of including modern high heat storage heaters in the eligible technologies list.

writtenQuestions.heading: VAT

writtenQuestions.heading: Fossil Fuels: Heating
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department is taking to ensure the transition away from fossil fuel heating involves low carbon solutions that suit the different types of UK housing stock.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that all homes are able to benefit from the transition to low carbon heating. That’s why the Warm Homes Plan supports a range of measures and technologies to suit the variety of homes across the country. Modern heat...

items.text: Name of donor: Parliamentary Renewable & Sustainable Energy Group (PRASEG) Secretariat for the APPG for Renewable & Sustainable Energy Address of donor: PRASEG, 10 Dean Farrar Street, London SW1H 0DX Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Accommodation, return flights and subsistence, value £808 Destination of visit: Denmark Dates of visit: 23 October 2025 to 24 October 2025 Purpose of visit: APPG visit to high-level meetings and site visits in Copenhagen cross-channel cooperation for decarbonising heat and energy. (Registered 1 November 2025)

Name of donor: Federal Department of Foreign Affairs, Switzerland Address of donor: Embassy of Switzerland in the UK, 16-18 Montagu Place, London W1H 2BQ Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Travel (£380), accommodation (£990), in-country travel (£290) and meals (£240), value £1,900 Destination of visit: Switzerland (Berne & Zurich) Dates of visit: 5 November 2025 to 8 November 2025 Purpose of visit: Cross Party MP delegation visit with focus on Switzerland’s rail and transport network and Vocational and Professional Education and Training (VPET) system. (Registered 16 November 2025)
